title: Feature workflow state
description: Schema for `.claude/features/<WORKSPACE>/state.json`, where `<WORKSPACE>` is a tracker ticket ID (ticket mode) or `_pr-<NUMBER>` (PR-only review mode). The durable progress record for a single feature workflow run.

ticket.description: Tracker ticket ID, e.g. ABC-1234. Pattern is configurable per project in .claude/commands/feature.md. Null in PR-only review mode, where review runs without a tracker ticket.

branch.description: Git branch created in Stage 3. Null before implementation begins.

stage.description: Current stage. Workflow resumes from here on re-invocation.

owns_branch.description: True if the head branch lives in this repo (not a fork) and we can push to it. Written explicitly by the conductor in both modes — Stage 4 (open PR) sets it true after creating the branch in ticket mode; the PR-only review entry computes it from the ownership check (same-repo head AND `gh pr checkout` success). Stage 5's auto-fix loop gates on a strict `=== true` check, so this field MUST be present whenever the loop runs.

review_mode.description: Discriminator the review-fix loop reads to choose its coordination channel. 'in_place' (default): reviewers and pr-triage post inline comments/replies on the PR and the fixer pushes to the PR head branch — the existing behavior for ticket review and PR-comment review. 'stacked': non-invasive mode (`/feature-review #<PR> --stacked`); the target PR is never mutated (no comments posted, no commits pushed). The reviewer↔triage↔fixer loop coordinates entirely via workspace files under `.claude/features/_pr-<N>/` (see references/lane-tags.md, 'Workspace-file coordination'), and the fixes are delivered as a separate PR described by `delivery` below.

exit_reason.description: How the loop exited. In-place mode (`review_mode = 'in_place'`) uses 'clean': will_fix empty, PR ready to merge; 'unpushable': head branch is on a fork or otherwise un-pushable, will-fix surfaced as a punch list; 'max_rounds_exhausted': hit max_rounds with unresolved will-fix, in modes where we own the branch the user can still choose to iterate. Stacked mode (`review_mode = 'stacked'`) uses 'delivered': the loop finished (cleanly OR after hitting the round cap) and the separate delivery PR was opened — the target PR was never mutated. Whether the cap was hit is conveyed by `delivery.capped`, NOT by a separate exit reason. Null while the loop is in progress.

delivery.description: Stacked-mode only (`review_mode = 'stacked'`). Records the separate, reviewable PR that carries the agreed fixes. Null in 'in_place' mode and until the delivery PR is opened. Every field is nullable until delivery happens.

branch.description: Name of the delivery branch the fixer commits to. Never the target PR's head branch — the target PR is read-only in stacked mode.

pr_url.description: URL of the delivery PR carrying the fixes. Null until the delivery PR is opened.

pr_number.description: Number of the delivery PR. Null until opened.

targets_head_branch.description: True if the delivery branch is stacked directly on the target PR's head branch (push-accessible in our repo) and the delivery PR targets that head branch, so the author sees only the proposed fixes. False for the fork-fallback: the target head is on a fork / not push-accessible, so the delivery branch is created off our base branch and the delivery PR links back to the target PR instead. Null until the base decision is made.

target_pr_number.description: Number of the original PR under review (the target). The delivery is stacked on / linked to this PR. Null until known.

capped.description: True if the loop hit `max_rounds` with unresolved must-fix items still outstanding. In stacked mode the delivery PR is opened anyway, with the unresolved must-fix items listed prominently as a punch list in its description (non-convergence is delivered with caveats, not blocked). Null until the loop exits.

bot_identity.description: GitHub login of the agent running this workflow (from `gh api user --jq .login`). Captured at workspace seed time. Used by pr-triage to verify that 'Fix verification failed' replies were actually authored by us — otherwise a third party could spoof verification failures on a public-repo PR and trick the fix subagent into applying bogus 'fixes'.

valid_thread_ids.description: Allowlist whose meaning depends on `review_mode`. In `in_place` mode it holds GraphQL review-thread node IDs that actually belong to this PR, populated by pr-review-orchestrator's pre-flight from `pullRequest.reviewThreads`. In `stacked` mode it instead holds workspace finding ids (the `id` field of each entry in `<WS>/findings.json`), populated from that file — the key name is kept for schema continuity across modes. In both modes, reviewer Step A, pr-triage, and the fix subagent MUST validate every id against this allowlist before acting on it (in_place: before calling resolveReviewThread / unresolveReviewThread; stacked: before applying a finding) — otherwise a compromised subagent could mutate threads or apply findings from an unrelated PR/run in the same org.

monitoring.description: Interactive stacked-review only (`review_mode = 'stacked'` plus the `--interactive` flag). Durable bookkeeping for the Slack-coordinated, comment-driven monitoring loop on the delivery PR. Null in `in_place` mode and in plain `--stacked` runs; every field is nullable/defaulted so existing states validate unchanged. Not in `required`. The loop reuses `bot_identity` (above) to filter the agent's own comments — no separate field. All loop state persists here so the re-entrant, one-cycle-per-invocation poll loop survives process restarts and resumes from persisted state.

channel_id.description: Slack channel ID extracted from the `archives/<ID>` segment of the thread permalink by the conductor. Null until extracted (or if unextractable — target validity is proven by access, not format).

thread_ts.description: Slack thread timestamp extracted from the permalink's trailing `p<digits>` token (decimal inserted 6 digits from the end) or a `thread_ts` query parameter. Null until extracted.

poll_minutes.description: Minutes between monitoring poll cycles. Resolved from the `--poll` flag overriding this default; persisted so a resumed loop keeps the same cadence.

idle_deadline_minutes.description: Minutes of inactivity after which a one-time idle Slack heads-up fires. Resolved from the `--idle` flag overriding this default; persisted so a resumed loop keeps the same cadence.

last_activity_at.description: Timestamp of the last handled human comment. Updated when a new human comment is processed; drives the idle-deadline check. Null until the first activity.

idle_pinged.description: True once the one-time idle heads-up has been posted for the current idle window. Reset to false when a new human comment arrives, so the ping survives restarts without re-firing every cycle or never firing.

handled_comment_ids.description: GitHub comment IDs already processed by the loop. New comments are deduped against this list each cycle; an edited handled comment is re-opened as unselected via content hash / updated_at rather than silently skipped.

awaiting_choice.description: Comments that have been replied to with multiple-choice options and are awaiting an unambiguous author selection. A selection is recognized only when exactly one valid token is present in the reply.

comment_id.description: GitHub comment ID this awaiting-choice entry tracks.

options.description: The concrete multiple-choice options posted for the comment author to select from.

reply_token_prefix.description: Token prefix (e.g. `option:`) that prefixes each posted option; a reply must contain exactly one valid token under this prefix to be a selection.

ignored_bot_authors.description: Configurable GitHub logins to skip when fetching new comments (in addition to `user.type == 'Bot'` and the agent's own `bot_identity`). Defaults to empty; bot logins are configured per project, never baked into core prose.

github_to_slack_handles.description: Best-effort map from GitHub username to Slack user ID (e.g. `U01ABC234`, the form a connector needs to render a real @-mention — not an `@handle`) for @-mentioning the PR author. Null when no mapping is configured; if absent or missing an entry, post the GitHub username as plain text with no @-mention — never block the loop on a missing mapping.

asset.description: Path to the stage's output artifact (brief.md, tasks.md, etc.).

thread_id.description: GraphQL review-thread node ID. Required so the fix subagent can call resolveReviewThread after the fix lands on the remote.

path.description: File path the comment anchors to. Required — the fix subagent uses path:line as the primary fix locator.

line.description: Line in the new file. Required — see path.
